skill.name: vector-database-engineer
skill.description: Expert in vector databases, embedding strategies, and semantic search implementation. Masters Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, and pgvector for RAG applications, recommendation systems, and similar
skill.summary: Expert guidance for vector database architecture, embedding optimization, and semantic search systems
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation text with no executable code, network requests, or security risks. The 'external_commands' flag was triggered by the word 'open' in a documentation sentence, not actual command execution. This is a legitimate educational skill about vector database engineering.

content.value_statement: Implement production-ready vector databases and semantic search. This skill provides expert guidance on embedding strategies, index optimization, and RAG architecture for modern AI applications.
actual_capabilities: Design vector database architecture for RAG applications
Select and optimize embedding models for specific use cases
Configure index types (HNSW, IVF, PQ) for performance
Implement hybrid search combining vector and keyword queries
Design document chunking strategies with overlap
Optimize vector search latency and recall tradeoffs
limitations: Cannot directly execute vector database queries
Does not install or configure vector database software
Cannot access external vector database APIs
Limited to guidance and code generation, not runtime execution

use_cases.title: Build a RAG knowledge base
use_cases.description: Design semantic search over documentation for AI-powered question answering

use_cases.title: Implement recommendation engine
use_cases.description: Create similarity-based product recommendations using vector embeddings

use_cases.title: Optimize vector search performance
use_cases.description: Tune indexing and chunking strategies for millions of vectors

prompt_templates.title: Select a vector database
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between Pinecone, Weaviate, and Qdrant for a document search system with 1 million vectors
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing the right vector database for your use case

prompt_templates.title: Design embedding strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an embedding pipeline for technical documentation. Recommend chunking size, overlap, and model selection
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning how to convert documents to vectors

prompt_templates.title: Configure HNSW index
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure HNSW index parameters for 90% recall at under 50ms latency on 5 million vectors
prompt_templates.scenario: Tuning index performance for your requirements

prompt_templates.title: Implement hybrid search
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement hybrid search combining vector similarity with keyword filters for product search
prompt_templates.scenario: Building search with both semantic and exact matching

output_examples.input: How should I chunk 500-page PDFs for semantic search?
output_examples.output: Use recursive character text splitting with 1000-1500 character chunks and 200 character overlap. This preserves context while maintaining semantic coherence. For technical documents, consider structure-aware chunking that respects section boundaries.

output_examples.input: Compare Pinecone vs Weaviate for production
output_examples.output: Pinecone offers managed scalability with zero operational overhead but has vendor lock-in. Weaviate provides self-hosted flexibility with hybrid search built-in but requires infrastructure management. Choose Pinecone for rapid development, Weaviate for cost control at scale.

best_practices: Always test embedding models on your specific domain before production deployment
Start with simple chunking strategies before optimizing for complex document structures
Monitor vector drift and plan periodic re-embedding cycles
Use metadata filtering to reduce search space before vector queries
anti_patterns: Using larger embedding dimensions without testing if smaller models work for your use case
Chunking documents without overlap, losing context between segments
Skipping recall testing and only measuring latency
Storing embeddings without their source text or metadata references

faq.question: What is the difference between HNSW and IVF indexing?
faq.answer: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) provides faster queries with higher memory usage. IVF (Inverted File) uses less memory but has slower query speed. Use HNSW for real-time applications, IVF for cost-sensitive large-scale deployments.

faq.question: How do I choose embedding dimensions?
faq.answer: Higher dimensions (1536) capture more semantic nuance but increase storage and latency. Start with 384-768 dimensions for most use cases. Only use 1536 if you have complex semantic relationships and sufficient infrastructure budget.

faq.question: Should I use pre-filtering or post-filtering for metadata?
faq.answer: Pre-filtering reduces search space and improves performance but may miss relevant results. Post-filtering ensures recall but wastes computation on filtered results. Use pre-filtering for strict constraints, post-filtering for soft preferences.

faq.question: What vector database should I use?
faq.answer: Pinecone for managed simplicity, Weaviate for hybrid search features, Qdrant for performance and filtering, pgvector if you already use PostgreSQL. Choose based on your team's expertise and infrastructure preferences.

faq.question: How do I handle embedding drift?
faq.answer: Embedding drift occurs when your data distribution changes over time. Monitor search quality metrics monthly and schedule quarterly re-embedding for critical applications. Use A/B testing to compare old and new embeddings before full migration.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to directly query my vector database?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and code generation for vector database architecture and optimization. It does not execute queries or connect to your database directly. You must implement the suggested code in your application.
